This advisory has been withdrawn because it is a duplicate of GHSA-r39h-4c2p-3jxp. This link is maintained to preserve external references.

### Original Description
OpenClaw before 2026.4.23 contains an arbitrary code execution vulnerability in the bundled plugin setup resolver that loads setup-api.js from process.cwd() during provider setup metadata resolution. Attackers can execute arbitrary JavaScript under the current user account by placing a malicious extensions/<plugin>/setup-api.js file in a repository and convincing a user to run OpenClaw commands from that directory.", "ghsa_id": "GHSA-xpr6-2hgm-4wwp", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-05-18T15:31:18Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-xpr6-2hgm-4wwp", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-xpr6-2hgm-4wwp"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-05-11T18:16:40Z", "published_at": "2026-05-11T18:31:47Z", "references": ["https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/security/advisories/GHSA-r39h-4c2p-3jxp", "https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-45004", "https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/commit/993781e6e6eaf50f033cfc3e3bf4f47059740707", "https://www.vulncheck.com/advisories/openclaw-arbitrary-code-execution-via-setup-api-js-in-current-working-directory", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-xpr6-2hgm-4wwp"], "repository_advisory_url": null, "severity": "high", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw", "summary": "Duplicate Advisory: OpenClaw vulnerable to arbitrary code execution via attacker-controlled setup-api.js loaded from cwd during env-key resolution", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-05-18T15:31:19Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-xpr6-2hgm-4wwp", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "2026.4.23", "package": {"ecosystem": "npm", "name": "openclaw"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "< 2026.4.23"}], "withdrawn_at": "2026-05-18T15:31:18Z"}

reasons: 資産不一致または判定情報不足

title: Duplicate Advisory: OpenClaw vulnerable to arbitrary code execution via attacker-controlled setup-api.js loaded from cwd during env-key resolution
